title: cuscuta liliputana
intro: Cuscuta liliputana, also known as dwarf dodder, is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the vascular tissues of its host plant. Cuscuta liliputana has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral activities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Cuscuta liliputana can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies, particularly in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine.
